CHICAGO. March 1S.-Members
of the interstate commerce COM
mission are here today for the pur-
pose of continuing the hearing on
the Spokane rate case. undoubtedly
the most important and far reach
ing in its effects of any ever heard
by the body of rate regulators, The
final decision of the commission
will affect all of the great trans
continental roads and, should II be
in favor of Spokane. all the interior
towns of the west will benefit.


The principal question involved
is whether the interstate commerce
commission is authorized by the
rate law to reduce rates. not alone
on a single commodity, but on all
commodities from the east to Spo-
kane and other cities of the Inland
Empire. 1t involves the adjust
ment and regulation of freight
tariffs between localities in a
broader sense than ever before at
tempted by the commission.


The municipal government and
chamber of commerce of Spokane
are plaintiffs in the case and the
Northern Pacific and Great North
ern railways. defendants. The Pa
cific Coast Jobbers' association
composed Of wholesale merchants
and shippers of the coast cities, has
interviewed in the case in favor O.
the railroads, declaring that the
rates as now constituted are JUS
and should not be changed.


The reason for filing the Spokane
case arises from the fact that The
freight rates 10 that city from New
York. Chicago. Minneapolis ant
other shipping centers of the cas-
and middle west are higher thar
the rates over the same roads tc
Seattle and other Pacific coas'
cities, despite the fact that the
latter are some 300 miles farther
west than Spokane. At present
Spokane pays the frelSht rate II
the Pacific seaboarG, plus the loca
freight rate back TO Spokane from
the coast.


Both the Great Northern and the
Northern Pacific have filed answers
defending the present rates on the
novel ground that on the Pacific
coast they are compelled TO NEE
water competition. and in order t(
do this are compelled to "breal
even" by holding up" the interio
towns. Stripped of its lega
phraseology, this is about what thu
answer of the railroad attorney!
amounts TO. The iobbers of thu
coast are naturally defending tn
present rates. since they benefi
largely by the arrangement


In addition to the session whicl
began today at Chicago, the com
mission will hold another hearin
on April 10 at Portland, Ore. Argr
ments of the attorneys for the dit
ferent interests represented in tn
case will be held before the con
mission in this city on June 8. 1t
likely that not less than two week
will be occupied in hearing tn
testimony and arguments.
